summarization.a: The figure shows the film thickness of a material over cycles, with a plateau at 200 Å until cycle 60 due to lack of full ALE cycle, followed by a linear decrease at a rate of 3.1 Å per cycle.
summarization.b: The figure shows the film thickness of a material at the transition between the two dosing gases over cycles.

a.question: How can the EPC be determined?
a.answer: a	By performing a linear fit in the last section of the graph and taking the slope.

a.question: Is the synergy high for this process?
a.answer: a	Synergy is high if one of the two doses on there own do not etch. This seems the case so the synergy is high.

a.question: How long would it take to etch 12 nm?
a.answer: a	12 nm, or 120 angstrom would require 39 cycles.

b.question: Why is there a drop in thickness after the switch from the plasma to the TMA step?
b.answer: b	Between these data points actually one full ALE cycle has taken place. This is also the reason that the thickness difference is 3 angstroms.
